food_pairing.name: Mosel Kabinett with Bavarian schweinshaxe
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Mosel Riesling Kabinett with roast pork knuckle and crackling
food_pairing.description: The Kabinett's racy acidity and low alcohol cut the fat of a roast pork knuckle while gentle sweetness flatters the crackling and apple-sauce side.

food_pairing.name: Mosel Auslese with obatzda
food_pairing.pairing: Fruity-sweet Mosel Riesling Auslese with the spiced Bavarian cheese spread obatzda
food_pairing.description: Honeyed Auslese sweetness tames the paprika and ripe-cheese funk of obatzda, while piercing acidity keeps the pairing fresh.

food_pairing.name: Mosel Kabinett with currywurst
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Mosel Riesling Kabinett with Berlin currywurst
food_pairing.description: Curry-ketchup spice and the snap of grilled sausage meet their match in a low-alcohol Kabinett whose sweetness cools the heat and acidity slices the fat.

food_pairing.name: Dry Mosel GG with eisbein
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Mosel Riesling Grosses Gewachs with boiled pork knuckle eisbein
food_pairing.description: A powerful dry Grosses Gewachs has the mineral structure and racy acidity to cut through the gelatinous richness of eisbein with sauerkraut.

food_pairing.name: Mosel Spatlese with Konigsberger Klopse
food_pairing.pairing: Fruity-style Mosel Riesling Spatlese with caper-cream meatballs Konigsberger Klopse
food_pairing.description: The Spatlese's sweetness and acidity balance the tangy caper-and-cream sauce, while its stone fruit lifts the delicate veal meatballs.

food_pairing.name: Mosel Kabinett with weisswurst
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Mosel Riesling Kabinett with Munich weisswurst and sweet mustard
food_pairing.description: A morning-friendly low-alcohol Kabinett echoes the sweet mustard and refreshes the palate against the delicate veal-and-parsley sausage.

food_pairing.name: Mosel Auslese with foie gras
food_pairing.pairing: Noble-sweet Mosel Riesling Auslese with seared foie gras
food_pairing.description: The classic sweet-Riesling-and-foie-gras match: botrytis sweetness and cutting acidity balance the rich liver without cloying, a fixture on Berlin's fine-dining wine lists.

food_pairing.name: Dry Saar Riesling with river trout
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Saar Riesling with pan-fried Mosel river trout
food_pairing.description: Bright slate-driven Saar acidity and citrus mirror the brown-butter and lemon of the local freshwater trout, the quintessential Mosel lunch.
